Industries: Artificial Intelligence (AI), Drones, Information Technology, Military, Software
Description: Helsing develops AI-powered defense tech, focusing on drones and software, to enhance military capabilities for democratic nations.
Top 5 Investors: General Catalyst, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Accel, BDT & MSD Partners, Greenoaks
Full Description: Helsing offers artificial intelligence solutions designed to support defence operations across land, air, naval, and subsurface domains. It provides software-driven platforms that integrate with existing military hardware to enable autonomous target detection, coordination, and engagement. Key offerings include a reconnaissance-and-strike system that fuses battlefield data, an AI‑enabled strike drone capable of operating in electronic warfare environments, underwater autonomous surveillance drones paired with an edge‑AI classifier, and AI “wingman” pilots for aircraft. Helsing collaborates with governments and industry to embed its AI-driven software into defense ecosystems while maintaining structured ethical oversight over technology use.
Founders: Gundbert Scherf, Niklas Köhler, Torsten Reil

Full Description: TEKEVER is a UAS manufacturer offering surveillance-as-a-service solutions that deliver actionable data and intelligence. Description: Roark Aerospace are the world's largest deployer of counter drone hardware and the pioneers of drone detection as a service.
Full Description: Roark Aerospace have manufactured and deployed over 30,000 c-uas devices globally as of 2025 with plans for a further 200,000 through 2027. These devices perform various drone detection, identification and tracking services alongside a c-uas (soft-kill) element. Roark are the pioneers of the "drone detection as a service" product suite, offering zero cap-ex, subscription based services for the public and private sector, focusing on delivering the base layer of a global c-uas layer.
